title: Astranthium ciliatum
taxonomy: Astranthium ciliatum is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its composite flowers and the presence of receptacles, which are typical features of the Asteraceae family. Astranthium ciliatum is also classified as a member of the tribe Astranthieae, which is a subgroup within the Asteraceae family. Within the tribe, Astranthium ciliatum is further classified as a species within the genus Astranthium. The classification of Astranthium ciliatum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family.
uses: Astranthium ciliatum is known to have various medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat fever and inflammation. The plant's roots and leaves are believed to possess antibacterial and antiviral properties. In some cultures, Astranthium ciliatum is used as a treatment for respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. However, its medicinal uses should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.

intro: Astranthium ciliatum, also known as Western False Aster, is a flowering plant native to the western United States. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the relief of pain and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Astranthium ciliatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Astranthium ciliatum is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with infections and inflammatory conditions.
